entries.description: Operator set pathAwareCritique: advisory.
Registered session 1 (start_session, claude/anthropic/claude-fable-5); read spec, proposal, verdict (amendment 3), constitution preload.

Routed the empty-manifest design cluster (task_type=architecture; anthropic 400 x3 -> excluded, rerouted). Ruling: reader-level null->[] acceptance; no new classification union member (valid-empty = present with zero entries); textual empty-line replacement with the parse-after-append guard as backstop. Saved raw: s1-empty-manifest-architecture.json.

readModulesManifest: bare `modules:` (YAML null) now reads as a valid empty manifest ([]), silently, exactly like `modules: []`; missing/wrong-typed modules key keeps the warn+degrade path. classifyModulesManifest doc updated; authoring flows inherit empty-parity via pickModuleForAuthoring (no QuickPick, no stamp, no notice).

scaffoldNewModule: valid-empty manifests grow via replaceEmptyModulesList (top-level empty `modules:` line replaced with the first block-style entry, trailing comment preserved); parse-after-append guard retained as backstop and now passes the empty->first-entry transition; refusal wording updated for the shapes that still refuse.

MODULES_YAML_TEMPLATE exported: Set 087 header comments + commented example entries + `modules: []` (gpt-5-4 adopted shape). Round-trip test: classifies valid-empty, appender grows it into a one-module manifest preserving all comments. Wiring into scaffold/ensure-write stays Set 094.

Test matrix: classification (absent/invalid x5/empty-flow/empty-null/populated), appender transitions (both empty forms, trailing comment, no-EOL, template), all-dropped refusals (block + flow style, ruling mandate 3), populated flow-style refusal, pickModuleForAuthoring empty-parity, copyPlanningPrompt byte-identical to no-manifest baseline, importPlanFromFile repo-level destination, bare-modules attribution/byte-stability pin on readSessionSets, runNewModuleFlow over the template.

Build + full suite green: extension unit/Layer 2 1368 passing; Layer 1 pytest 2922 passed / 6 skipped; Layer 3 Playwright 20 passed (npm run test:playwright, L-064-12). Lint: 7 pre-existing errors in files this session did not touch (identical on the pristine tree via stash check); touched files 0 errors.

Cross-provider verification (gpt-5-6): R1 Major (root-indented empty forms not appendable) -> fixed (indent captured, block re-indented, e2e tests); R2 Major (nested modules: key could swallow the replacement) -> fixed (one candidate per matching line, each validated against the parse-after-append guard, ROOT list wins, e2e tests); R3 Major (explicit null spellings accepted by reader but not appendable) -> fixed (regex mirrors YAML 1.2 core null spellings, e2e tests incl. nUll-stays-invalid). Suite re-run green after each fix.

R4 Major (quoted keys + multiline empty flow) DISPUTED as edge-case exhaustion (fresh serialization of the same semantic state each round; prescribed CST fix re-litigates the settled routed architecture ruling; failure mode is a loud no-write refusal). Constitution path: third-provider opinion (gemini-pro, saved raw at s1-third-opinion-empty-forms.json) -> ADJUDICATED-MINOR RESIDUAL: quoted-key hardening applied + test-pinned (quote style preserved); exotic serializations refuse loudly by design; residual documented and deferred to Sets 092-094 manifest guardrails.

R5 with the cross-round issue ledger + adjudication in the conventions block: VERIFIED, zero findings. Unit suite 1372 passing; tsc + eslint clean on touched files; dist rebuilt. Total verification cost ~$0.74 across 5 rounds.

disposition.json authored (status completed, verdict VERIFIED preserved, next_orchestrator per routed analysis: claude-code/anthropic/claude-opus-4-8/medium, adjudication recorded, lessons cited L-064-8 L-064-9 L-065-1); ai-assignment actuals updated; commit + push + close_session + notification.

Registered session 2 (start_session, claude/anthropic/claude-fable-5); read spec, S1 disposition/ai-assignment, verdict amendment 2, the Q8 matrix in both consensus files, and the constitution preload; mapped the S2 seams (SessionSetsModel.groupByModule, fileSystem module attribution, the three writer paths for the never-persist audit).

Routed the visible-module design cluster (task_type=architecture; anthropic 400 x3 -> excluded, S1 precedent). Round 1 (gpt-5-4, s2-visible-module-architecture.json) ruled Q1 (pure computeVisibleModules(classification, allSets, {legacyRootPlanExists}) in SessionSetsModel.ts; structured VisibleModule output with semantic warning codes) then truncated; continuation round (gemini-pro, s2-visible-module-architecture-2.json) ruled Q2-Q7. Synthesis note: the continuation's Q2 presence predicate (pseudo iff unstamped OR legacy plan) is AMENDED by the operator-confirmed Q8 matrix rows 'no manifest, no sets -> sole pseudo Default' / 'empty manifest, no sets -> sole pseudo' (the settled record outranks the advisory ruling): pseudo appears iff unstamped sets exist OR legacyRootPlanExists OR no other module group is visible. All other rulings adopted: fallback groups do not count as declared for presence but DO count as coexisting for Unassigned naming; declared-but-empty modules always visible; fallback groups alphabetical, after declared, before pseudo, displayName = raw slug; module: default with no declared default slug = fallback group, never merged; never-persist guard = pseudo-selection writes no module: key + declared literal default stamps module: default legitimately; legacy root-plan path is an exported constant always carried on the pseudo element.

computeVisibleModules exported from SessionSetsModel.ts (pure; classification + SessionSet[] + {legacyRootPlanExists} -> ordered VisibleModule[] with kind declared|fallback|pseudo, structured warning codes, planPath). Declared modules render in manifest order including zero-set entries; fallback groups one per undeclared raw stamp (alphabetical, undeclared-slug warning, never hidden); pseudo-module last, present iff unstamped sets OR legacy root plan OR otherwise-empty tree (Q8-amended predicate). Attribution re-derived from config.module against the classification so the function is total over its inputs. NOT consumed by any render path (Set 092).

Naming rule: PSEUDO_MODULE_SOLE_NAME 'Default' when the pseudo-module is the only visible group; PSEUDO_MODULE_COEXIST_NAME 'Unassigned' once any declared or fallback group coexists (routed ruling Q3: fallback groups count). A declared literal `default` slug renders as a normal declared module and forces Unassigned (pinned).

Writer audit + guard tests over all three module: writer paths (consumerBootstrap {{MODULE_LINE}}, sessionGenPrompt exemplar stamp, planImport prompt) plus scaffoldNewModule and pickModuleForAuthoring: pseudo-module resolution can never emit a module: line; only a picked manifest entry is ever stamped (declared literal `default` stamps legitimately); reader treats hand-written module: default with no declared default slug as an undeclared stamp -> fallback group, warning included, never merged into the pseudo-module (end-to-end from-disk test).

LEGACY_ROOT_PLAN_REL = docs/planning/project-plan.md exported from moduleAuthoring.ts; always carried as the pseudo-module's planPath (existence is the consumer's separate check, ruling Q7); declared modules resolve through modulePlanRelPath; fallback groups carry null. Wizard modules' equal local literals deliberately untouched (byte-stability) - unification named as Set 093 work in the compat matrix.

docs/planning/work-explorer-compat-matrix.md authored: the nine verbatim Q8 rows + cross-cutting rows (legacy-plan visibility, declared-but-empty, literal-default, hand-written default, ordering, plan mappings, never-persist guard, byte-stability), each row citing its pinning test in visibleModules.test.ts by name; per-set consumption notes for 092/093/094 including the S1 adjudicated-minor residual handoff to 092 guardrails.

Round 1 (gpt-5-6): ISSUES_FOUND, 2 Major. (1) compat-matrix row 9 overstated pseudo-module presence for invalid manifests - row reworded to the standard presence rule + new pinning test 'Q8 invalid-manifest-all-stamped' (fallback groups only, no pseudo). (2) never-persist guard tests too weak - pseudo-path assertions strengthened to reject ANY module stamp/wording (not just 'module: default'); session-gen stamp uniqueness asserted; two provenance integration tests added coupling pickModuleForAuthoring to all three writers (absent manifest -> none -> zero module output everywhere; declared literal 'default' -> auto-picked entry -> exactly that slug stamped, operator-visible notice). Unit suite 1407 passing.

Round 2 (gpt-5-6): ISSUES_FOUND, 2 fresh Major (both matrix-contract gaps, distinct from R1's). (1) empty-manifest x stamped-set states undocumented - matrix rows 5a/5b added (all-stamped: fallback only, no manifest-missing; stamped+unstamped: fallback + pseudo Unassigned with unstamped-sets) with two named pinning tests. (2) legacy-plan cross-cutting row over-promised 'no warning' - reworded to the standard warning precedence (legacy plan keeps the pseudo VISIBLE but never masks manifest faults) + a three-classification pinning test (absent->manifest-missing, invalid->manifest-invalid, valid-empty->quiet). Unit suite 1410 passing. Ledger note: R1 and R2 findings are all fixed; nothing disputed, nothing resurrected.

Round 3 (gpt-5-6): 1 fresh Major - 'declared-default guard unproven for the plan-import writer' (demands a module: default frontmatter pin on buildPlanningPrompt / end-to-end inspection of generated spec.md). Second fresh same-class Major (guard completeness) -> DISPUTED per the S1 calibration note. Implementable kernel applied first: the plan prompt's full output surface pinned exactly (verbatim scoping note with the picked slug, exact planPath destination, negative no-module: assertion); 38 suite tests green. Third-provider adjudication (gemini-pro, s2-third-opinion-plan-import-stamp.json): ADJUDICATED-MINOR-RESIDUAL, finding dismissed as resolved - the premise misidentified the writer (plan prompts carry no spec frontmatter by design; the two frontmatter writers are already literal-pinned with provenance; the demanded e2e is not automatable in-repo). Proceeding to the confirming round.

Round 4 (gpt-5-6): 1 fresh Major, new class (purity) - computeVisibleModules called modulePlanRelPath, which console.warns on an unsafe declared planPath, so the promised pure model function had an observable side effect. Fixed by splitting the resolver: new pure resolveModulePlanRelPath (returns {path, degraded} - degradation is data, no logging) consumed by computeVisibleModules; modulePlanRelPath stays as the warning wrapper for the interactive flows. Two new tests: a console.warn spy proving the computation is side-effect-free on unsafe plan paths, and the resolver/wrapper split pin. Compat matrix 'Declared plan resolution' row updated. tsc clean; unit suite 1411 passing; dist rebuilt.

Round 5 returned VERIFIED with two nits: abbreviated compat-matrix test citations and mutable VisibleModule.sets type. Fixed both nits; focused Set 091 S2 unit suite 39 passing; npx tsc --noEmit clean. The VS Code-hosted grep run compiled cleanly but failed before executing tests because out/media/session-sets-tree/gettingStartedHtml.js was not copied into the test out tree.
